subject: English
title: The Lost Child – Medium Short Notes

key_points: The story describes a child visiting a fair with his parents during a spring festival
The child is attracted to toys, sweets, balloons, flowers, and rides
His curiosity causes him to lag behind his parents
He becomes frightened when he realizes he has lost his parents
A kind stranger tries to comfort him with various attractions
characters: The Lost Child – Curious and innocent but emotionally dependent on his parents
Parents – Symbol of love, protection, and security
Stranger – A compassionate man who helps the child
literary_devices: Imagery – Vivid descriptions of nature and fair attractions
Symbolism – The fair represents material attraction
Contrast – Happiness at the beginning and fear later
exam_tips: Explain the change in the child’s priorities after he gets lost
Mention how the fair setting helps develop the theme
Focus on the emotional bond between parents and child
Use examples of attractions the child desires
